course_description:
This course will focus on the application of differential & integral calculus as well as matrix algebra to economic models. Simple unconstrained optimisation will be studied. The course will also introduce some aspects of multi-variate analysis.

This course introduces students to relevant mathematical methods and their application in economics. A good understanding of these methods is crucial for students to comprehend important concepts in other selected areas in economics.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

course_contacts.name: Student Enquiries, School of Economics
course_contacts.notes: All enquiries regarding student and academic administration (i.e. non-course content information, e.g., class allocation, timetables, extension to assessment due date, etc.) should be directed to enquiries@economics.uq.edu.au . Enquiries relating specifically to course content should be directed to econ7150@uq.edu.au .

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Workshops commence in Teaching Week 1.
Tutorials commence in Teaching Week 2.
Students are required to log their preferences for a tutorial group via My Timetable (available through my.UQ dashboard).
Students should refer to the timetable prior to the commencement of classes to ensure that they have the most up-to-date information, as late room changes may occur from time to time. The timetable can be downloaded here:
Public Timetable
.
Public Holidays:
Wed 14 August (Royal Queensland Show), Mon 7 October (King's Birthday).
In-Semester Break:
23 - 29 September. Semester 2 classes recommence Mon 30 September.
course_aims: This course provides an introduction to the mathematical tools commonly used in economic analysis.

learning_outcomes.description: Represent and manipulate simple economic models algebraically.
Use differential calculus, including partial derivatives and differentials, across a range of economic problems.
Apply integration techniques to economic concepts such as consumer surplus and producer surplus.
Apply methods of unconstrained and constrained optimization to simple economic problems.
Use matrix algebra to solve sets of simultaneous equations and simple input-output models.

assessment_details.title: Assignment 1
assessment_details.due_date: 6/09/2024 - 13/09/2024 Assignment 1 will be released at 10 am on Friday, 6 September, and is due by 4 pm on Friday, 13 September.
assessment_details.task_description: The assignment will be a series of problems that test students' knowledge of the topics covered in workshops and tutorials. Assignment 1 will cover material from Topics 1 to 6. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Assignments must be handwritten and legible. Scan the assignment (scanners are available at the Library) and create ONE PDF document. Examine your PDF document prior to submission to ensure it is legible and complete. Submit online through Learn.UQ (Blackboard) Turnitin. More details will be provided on Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions are limited to 7 calendar days to ensure timely feedback to other students.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Final Exam
assessment_details.task_description: The two-hour written exam will cover Topics 1 to 12, i.e., all topics from the course. The final exam will be held during the examination period at a time and place set by Central Exams. Important reminders and notices will be posted on Learn.UQ (Blackboard). This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). learning_activities.topic: No Tutorials

Workshop 1: Set Theory, Logic & Review of Algebra Essentials of set theory and logic; real numbers; exponents; algebra; inequalities; intervals. Readings: Ch 1 & Ch 2.1-2.7 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01
Tutorial 1 Set Theory, Logic & Review of Algebra Readings: Ch 1 & Ch 2.1-2.7 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01

Workshop 2: Summation Notation & Solving Equations Summation notation; reviewing methods of solving equations. Readings: Ch 2.8, 2.9, 2.11 & Ch 3 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 2.9, 2.10, 2.12 & Ch 3 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01
Tutorial 2 Summation Notation & Solving Equations Readings: Ch 2.8, 2.9, 2.11 & Ch 3 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 2.9, 2.10, 2.12 & Ch 3 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01

Workshop 3: Functions of One Variable Linear functions; quadratic functions; polynomials; power, exponential and logarithmic functions; graphs of functions; inverse functions. Readings: Ch 4 & Ch 5.1-5.3 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01
Tutorial 3 Functions of One Variable Readings: Ch 4 & Ch 5.1-5.3 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01
Workshop 4: Calculus 1 Basic concepts; rules of differentiation. Readings: Ch 6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Public Holiday: Royal Queensland Show Wednesday 14/08/2024 No tutorials on this day. Students are invited to attend another tutorial for this week only.
Tutorial 4 Calculus 1 Readings: Ch 6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Workshop 5: Calculus 2 Derivatives in Use - Implicit differentiation; linear and polynomial approximations; Taylor's formula Readings: Ch 7.1, 7.2, 7.4, 7.5, 7.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Tutorial 5 Calculus 2 Readings: Ch 7.1, 7.2, 7.4, 7.5, 7.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016 or 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Workshop 6: Calculus 3 Single-variable optimisation; determination of extreme points; local extreme points. Readings: Ch 8 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 8 & Ch 9 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
Tutorial 6 Calculus 3 Readings: Ch 8 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 8 & Ch 9 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

Workshop 7: Calculus 4 Partial derivatives; multivariate optimisation. Readings: Ch 11.1-11.2 & Ch 13.1-13.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 14.1-14.2 & Ch 17.1-17.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
Tutorial 7 Calculus 4 Readings: Ch 11.1-11.2 & Ch 13.1-13.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 14.1-14.2 & Ch 17.1-17.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

Workshop 8: Calculus 5 Lagrange multiplier; introduction to constrained optimisation; applications. Readings: Ch 14.1-14.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 18.1-18.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L02, L04
Tutorial 8 Calculus 5 Readings: Ch 14.1-14.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 18.1-18.5 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L02, L04

Workshop 9: Calculus 6 Basic concepts of integration; indefinite and definite integrals; integration by parts; integration by substitution. Readings: Ch 9.1-9.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 10.1-10.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L03
In-Semester Break No workshop, no tutorials, and no consultations this week.
Tutorial 9 Calculus 6 Readings: Ch 9.1-9.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 10.1-10.6 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L03

Workshop 10: Matrix Algebra 1 and 2 Introduction to linear algebra: matrix notation; matrix and vector operations. Simultaneous equations; Gaussian elimination; determinants; minors and cofactors. Readings: Ch 15-16 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 12-13 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L05
Public Holiday: King's Birthday Monday 7/10/2024 No workshop and no tutorials this week due to the King's Birthday public holiday.
Tutorial 10 Matrix Algebra 1 & 2 Readings: Ch 15-16 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 12-13 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L05

Workshop 11: Matrix Algebra 3 Cramer's rule; solving simultaneous equations; adjoint method for finding an inverse. Readings: Ch 15-16 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 12-13 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L05
Tutorial 11 Matrix Algebra 3 Readings: Ch 15-16 (Sydsæter et al., 2016) or Ch 12-13 (Sydsæter et al., 2021) Learning outcomes: L01, L05
Workshop 12: Review Exam preparation and a review of the course content. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
